tags.name: Mill Channel new cleaning station
tags.marine_life: mantas, cleaning station, grey reef sharks, whitetip reef sharks
tags.notes: New station further inside lagoon; shallower than old station (~5m/16ft), lower visibility than old station but extended bottom time possible

tags.name: Mill Channel old cleaning station
tags.marine_life: mantas, soft corals, cleaning station, grey reef sharks, eagle rays
tags.notes: Original cleaning station at ~18m/60ft, closer to outer reef edge with clearer blue water; NDL-limited dives; less-dived than new station

tags.name: Mill Channel: Ridge
tags.marine_life: mantas (up to 30 sighted at once), jacks, black snappers, whitetip reef sharks, grey reef sharks
tags.notes: Tzimoulis Ridge - 30ft (9m) coral ridge across Mi'l Channel that intensifies tidal current creating natural cleaning station; 'Mi'l' means 'strong current' in Yapese

tags.name: Vertigo
tags.marine_life: grey reef sharks and blacktip reef sharks (10-40 per dive), whitetip reef sharks, groupers, snappers, moray eels
tags.notes: Wall starts at ~5m and plunges over 60m. Shark feeding program operated here for 25+ years. Wall covered in crinoids and small sponges. Located south of Mi'l Channel on west coast.

tags.name: Zero plane
tags.aircraft_type: uncertain - initially identified as Japanese Zero, but may be Mitsubishi G4M Betty (two-seater); unconfirmed
tags.history: WWII Japanese aircraft, bullet holes visible in wings; identity unconfirmed
tags.notes: Aircraft identity disputed: Manta Ray Bay Resort notes it 'was said to be a Japanese Zero, however...a two-seater...claimed it's a Betty.' No definitive identification confirmed from sources.

No independent sources confirm 'Manta Ray Bay' as a named dive site distinct from the resort. The resort name 'Manta Ray Bay Resort' does not correspond to a documented manta feeding bay dive site. Stammtisch and Valley of the Rays are the named manta sites in Goofnuw Channel; Mi'l Channel cleaning stations are the named sites on the northwest. Could not validate as a distinct site. Marked unresolved.

tags.features: caverns, pinnacles, chasms, swim-throughs, chimneys with sunbeam light shafts; white sand bottom
tags.marine_life: whitetip reef sharks, grey reef sharks, bumphead parrotfish (50+ schools), eagle rays, sea turtles, stingrays, morays, tuna, amberjack, porcelain crabs, dragonettes, nudibranchs, leaf fish, scorpionfish, ghost pipefish, lionfish
tags.notes: Depth corrected from 20m to 30m per mantaray.com. Occasional whale shark sightings reported. Rich macro life confirmed by multiple sources. 'Strongest diversity at any single dive site' in Yap per mantaray.com.

tags.features: vertical wall to 40m (130ft), then slopes to 60m+ ledge; soft daisy corals, crinoids, Bartlett's Fairy Basslets
tags.marine_life: lionfish (numerous in crevices), mantas, eagle rays, turtles, grey reef sharks, large tuna, leaf fish, anemones; Bartlett's Fairy Basslets at wall edge
tags.notes: Confirmed at extreme southern tip of Yap's fringing reef. Described as 'one of the premier wall dives in the world' by mantaray.com. Current can be strong. Depth corrected from 25m to 40m (wall drops to 130ft before sloping).

tags.marine_life: squirrelfish, snappers, groupers, large schools of anthias, lionfish, clown triggerfish; angelfish, butterflyfish, parrotfish, reef sharks, barracudas, tuna, moray eels
tags.coral: large brown Porite coral with tube worms, daisy corals, yellow soft coral under ledges at ~21m depth
tags.notes: Located NW extension from Yap Caverns. Confirmed vertical wall with crevices and overhangs. No mandarin fish confirmed at this site by any source. Depth range corrected to 6-45m per mantaray.com.

tags.features: vertical walls on both sides of Nimpal Inlet entrance; pink and purple soft corals at 18m; black coral and deep-water sea fans below 36m
tags.marine_life: grey reef sharks, blacktip reef sharks (at wall top), crinoids (multiple species), unicornfish schools
tags.notes: Located at Nimpal Inlet entrance on western side of Yap. Boats cannot enter inlet for surface interval due to shallow entrance. Vertical wall extends from 10m to 80m+.

tags.history: damaged by November 1992 typhoon; reef recovering with hard corals intact; lettuce and staghorn corals most affected
tags.marine_life: schooling jacks, barracuda (hundreds), grey reef sharks, whitetip reef sharks, baby eagle rays at cleaning station
tags.coral_recovery: actively rejuvenating since typhoon damage; hard coral formations intact
tags.notes: Mandarin fish removed from this site - no source confirms mandarin fish at Magic Kingdom. Mandarin fish are confirmed at Rainbow Reef (near O'Keefe's Island), not here. Eagle ray cleaning station sighting confirmed by ScubaBoard trip report.

No independent sources found confirming 'Sunrise Reef' as a named dive site in Yap. Not listed on mantaray.com or visityap.com site listings. Site type and description remain based on regional characteristics. Marked unresolved.

No independent sources confirm 'Rainbow Wall' as a named dive site in Yap. Note: 'Rainbow Reef' is a confirmed inshore site near O'Keefe's Island, shallow (3-10m), famous for mandarin fish - entirely different from 'Rainbow Wall'. No wall dive by this name appears on mantaray.com or visityap.com. Marked unresolved.

tags.marine_life: garden eels, pipefish, ghost pipefish, nudibranchs (multiple species), symbiotic gobies and shrimps, mantis shrimp, hawksbill turtles, whip coral shrimp
tags.features: rubble bottom, sand slope, coral bommies; 10-minute ride from Manta Ray Bay Resort
tags.notes: Confirmed macro photography site with rubble bottom. DAN describes as 'gentle sand slope punctuated by hard-coral bommies'. Divessi/Scubago describes dive duration of 100 minutes possible.

No independent sources confirm 'Land's End' as a named dive site in Yap. Visityap.com lists 'End of the Land' (a near-vertical wall to ocean floor with soft corals and crinoids above 18m) which may correspond to this site, but the exact match is uncertain. Marked unresolved pending confirmation.
